message:
fix(vdbe,btree,core,mvcc,parser,pager): UPDATE FROM codegen, index seek fix, CAST/affinity corrections, and performance improvements

VDBE codegen (fsqlite-vdbe):
- Implement full UPDATE ... FROM codegen with nested-loop join strategy.
  Add codegen_update_from() generating OpenWrite for target, OpenRead for
  FROM source, dual Rewind/Next loops, WHERE filter, and delete+insert
  update pattern. Previously returned Unsupported error.
- Add SecondaryScan struct and secondary field to ScanCtx for multi-table
  column resolution in UPDATE ... FROM expressions. emit_expr now falls
  through to secondary table when column not found in primary.
- Add emit_column_from_cursor() helper for emitting Column/Rowid opcodes
  against a specific cursor.
- Fix If/IfNot opcodes to use integer truncation (to_integer) instead of
  float comparison, matching C SQLite's sqlite3VdbeIntValue() semantics
  where 0.5 is falsy.
- Fix sql_rem (modulo) to use f64 fmod semantics instead of integer
  truncation, matching C SQLite's OP_Remainder behavior.
- Switch HashMap/HashSet to hashbrown in engine.rs for performance.
- Update test from rejection assertion to positive nested-loop validation.

B-tree cursor (fsqlite-btree):
- Split index_seek into index_seek (for queries) and index_seek_for_insert
  (for mutations). The query path now handles right-edge leaf falloff by
  using advance_next to find the successor, while the insert path keeps
  the cursor anchored at the leaf for correct insertion context.
- Add proptest for index B-tree invariants with random insert/delete
  sequences (up to 2000 ops), verifying sorted order and element count.

Connection and core (fsqlite-core):
- Rewrite implicit_index_definitions_from_create_table_sql to parse AST
  ColumnConstraintKind directly instead of going through compat_persist
  string parsing. More robust for UNIQUE and PRIMARY KEY detection.
- Schema reload now returns an error instead of silently skipping when an
  index cannot be reconstructed, preventing silent data corruption.
- Fix type_to_affinity() priority order to match SQLite spec section 3.1:
  INT > TEXT/CHAR/CLOB > BLOB > REAL/FLOA/DOUB > NUMERIC (was previously
  checking REAL before TEXT).
- Rewrite apply_cast() to use SQLite affinity substring matching instead
  of exact type name matching. Add parse_integer_prefix and
  parse_float_prefix for SQLite-compatible prefix extraction from strings.
- Simplify GROUP_CONCAT and sqlite_value_to_text to use SqliteValue::to_text().
- Pass HashMap conversions via into_iter().collect() for autoincrement
  and column defaults (hashbrown compatibility).
- Add hashbrown workspace dependency.

MVCC write coordinator (fsqlite-mvcc):
- Simplify compat_commit_batch: eagerly reserve pages in the commit index
  during Phase 1 validation (under the write lock) instead of deferring
  to Phase 4. This eliminates intra-batch conflict tracking and the
  separate batch_page_owner HashMap, reducing code by ~70 lines.
- Remove now-unused validate_fcw_set, update_commit_index methods.
- Move allocate_commit_seq to a more logical position in the impl block.
- Drop the index write lock before Phase 3 I/O for better concurrency.

Parser (fsqlite-parser):
- Optimize lexer whitespace skipping: batch-scan whitespace length first,
  then advance_by() once instead of character-by-character advance().
- Add Scope::has_cte() for recursive CTE visibility checking through
  parent scope chain (previously only checked current scope).
- Fix ORDER BY alias resolution to use add_alias with pseudo-table
  instead of adding individual aliases, matching SQLite scoping rules.
- Use has_cte() consistently in bind_table_to_scope and resolve_table_name.

Pager (fsqlite-pager):
- Switch arc_cache, page_cache, and s3_fifo from std HashMap to hashbrown
  HashMap for better performance on cache-heavy workloads.
- Add hashbrown workspace dependency.

Types (fsqlite-types):
- Replace Mutex<Option<T>> with OnceLock<T> for eprocess_oracle and
  native_cx in CxInner. Eliminates lock contention on every cancellation
  check (hot path). clear_* methods become no-ops since OnceLock cannot
  be cleared, but these are unused in practice.

Other fixes:
- fsqlite-func: Use saturating_neg() in SubstrFunc to avoid panic on
  i64::MIN input.
- fsqlite-vfs: Downgrade io_uring conformal breach messages from warn to
  info level to reduce log noise during normal fallback behavior.
- fsqlite-wal: Optimize last_commit_frame() to scan frames in reverse
  (finding last commit in O(1) for common case instead of O(n)).
  Inline decode_u32_words and read_be_u32_at, use try_into().unwrap()
  instead of manual copy_from_slice.
- Cargo.toml: Enable debug=true and force-frame-pointers=true in release
  profile for profiling support (strip=false).

New integration tests:
- bd-2yqp6.6.1: SSI serialization anomaly differential suite comparing
  FrankenSQLite vs C SQLite conflict detection.
- bd-2yqp6.6.3: Crash recovery corruption matrix differential suite.
- fsqlite/tests/insert_test.rs: INSERT INTO ... SELECT cross-table test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:16:38.900607240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:32:52.940681030Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:39:47.115694828Z","close_reason":"DUPLICATE: Superseded by finer-grained bd-34de (§12.5-12.6) + bd-3kin (§12.7-12.9)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x2z","depends_on_id":"bd-257u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1x2z","depends_on_id":"bd-31t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":587,"issue_id":"bd-1x2z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §12.5-12.9 DDL: CREATE TABLE + INDEX + VIEW + TRIGGER + Other

### CREATE TABLE (§12.5)
`CREATE [TEMP] TABLE [IF NOT EXISTS] [schema.]name (column-def [, constraint]*) [WITHOUT ROWID] [STRICT]`.
Also: `CREATE TABLE ... AS select-stmt`.

**Column definition:** `name [type] [constraint]*`. Column constraints: PRIMARY KEY [ASC|DESC] [conflict] [AUTOINCREMENT], NOT NULL [conflict], UNIQUE [conflict], CHECK (expr), DEFAULT (expr|literal|number), COLLATE, REFERENCES (foreign key), [GENERATED ALWAYS] AS (expr) [STORED|VIRTUAL].

**Table constraints:** PRIMARY KEY (cols) [conflict], UNIQUE (cols) [conflict], CHECK (expr), FOREIGN KEY (cols) REFERENCES table [(cols)] [foreign-key-clause].

**Type affinity rules** (first match wins): 1. Contains "INT" → INTEGER. 2. Contains "CHAR"/"CLOB"/"TEXT" → TEXT. 3. Contains "BLOB" or empty → BLOB. 4. Contains "REAL"/"FLOA"/"DOUB" → REAL. 5. Otherwise → NUMERIC.

**WITHOUT ROWID:** Index B-tree clustered on PK. Requires explicit PK. No rowid pseudo-column, no AUTOINCREMENT, INTEGER PK ≠ rowid alias.

**STRICT (3.37+):** Column types restricted to INT/INTEGER/REAL/TEXT/BLOB/ANY. Type checking enforced on INSERT/UPDATE.

**Generated columns (3.31+):** VIRTUAL (computed on read, not stored, can't index directly). STORED (computed on write, stored, can be indexed). Cannot reference later generated columns.

**AUTOINCREMENT:** Only on INTEGER PRIMARY KEY. Uses sqlite_sequence table. Guarantees rowids never reused.

**Foreign key clause:** REFERENCES parent (col) [ON DELETE/UPDATE {SET NULL|SET DEFAULT|CASCADE|RESTRICT|NO ACTION}] [MATCH {SIMPLE|PARTIAL|FULL}] [[NOT] DEFERRABLE [INITIALLY DEFERRED|IMMEDIATE]]. SQLite parses MATCH but enforces only MATCH SIMPLE. Requires `PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON`.

### CREATE INDEX (§12.6)
`CREATE [UNIQUE] INDEX [IF NOT EXISTS] [schema.]name ON table (indexed-column [,...]) [WHERE expr]`.

**Partial indexes:** WHERE clause restricts rows. Planner uses only when query WHERE implies index WHERE.
**Expression indexes:** Index on computed expressions. Planner matches via AST structural equality after normalization.

### CREATE VIEW (§12.7)
`CREATE [TEMP] VIEW [IF NOT EXISTS] [schema.]name [(aliases)] AS select`. Expanded inline (not materialized). Read-only unless INSTEAD OF trigger defined.

### CREATE TRIGGER (§12.8)
`CREATE [TEMP] TRIGGER [IF NOT EXISTS] [schema.]name {BEFORE|AFTER|INSTEAD OF} {DELETE|INSERT|UPDATE [OF col,...]} ON table [FOR EACH ROW] [WHEN expr] BEGIN stmt; ... END`.

**Timing:** BEFORE (can modify/prevent), AFTER (post-DML), INSTEAD OF (views only).
**Pseudo-tables:** INSERT: NEW only. DELETE: OLD only. UPDATE: both OLD and NEW.
**WHEN clause:** Trigger body executes only if WHEN is true.
**Body:** Multiple DML statements. Can reference OLD, NEW, RAISE().
**Recursive triggers:** `PRAGMA recursive_triggers=ON`. Max depth SQLITE_MAX_TRIGGER_DEPTH (1000).

**Rust safety directive (CRITICAL):** Trigger execution MUST NOT use call-stack recursion. MUST use explicit heap-allocated frame stack (`Vec<VdbeFrame>`). MUST enforce capability-budgeted memory ceiling via Cx for nested frames.

### Other DDL (§12.9)
**ALTER TABLE:** RENAME TO, RENAME COLUMN, ADD COLUMN, DROP COLUMN (3.35+, always rewrites table).
**DROP:** TABLE, INDEX, VIEW, TRIGGER — all with IF EXISTS.

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:07:29.813257726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:32:52.988940372Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:39:57.334718214Z","close_reason":"DUPLICATE: Superseded by finer-grained bd-2tu6 (§10.1-10.2) + bd-18zh (§10.3-10.4)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x55","depends_on_id":"bd-1ik","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":588,"issue_id":"bd-1x55","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §10 Query Pipeline Overview

SQL text -> Lexer (memchr-accelerated, zero-copy spans) -> Parser (recursive descent, Pratt precedence) -> AST (strongly typed) -> Name Resolution (table/column binding, * expansion) -> Query Planning (index selection, cost, join ordering) -> VDBE Bytecode Generation (190+ opcodes) -> Execution (fetch-execute, match dispatch) -> Results (iterator of Row).

## §10.1 Lexer Detail

~150 TokenType variants. Each token carries TokenType + Span (byte range + line/col).

**Token categories:** Literals (Integer, Float, String, Blob, Variable), Identifiers (Id, QuotedId with DQS flag), Keywords (~120 KwXxx variants), Operators/punctuation (Plus through Concat), Special (Eof, Error).

**QuotedId note:** "hello" is ALWAYS QuotedId at lexer level (matching C SQLite tokenize.c:413). DQS legacy behavior (reinterpreting as string literal) handled in name resolution. QuotedId tokens carry EP_DblQuoted flag.

**Operator note:** Eq/EqEq and Ne/LtGt preserved as distinct tokens for diagnostics and pretty-printing, but parser treats each pair identically.

**Literal parsing:** String: single-quoted, '' escape, memchr-accelerated. Numbers: decimal, hex (0x prefix), float (.e/E). Blob: X'hex', must have even digit count. Error tokens for invalid input (unterminated string, bad hex, unrecognized char).

**Line/column tracking:** Maintains line/col counters. Tokens carry Span with byte offsets + (line, col) at start.

## §10.2 Parser Detail

Hand-written recursive descent (NOT generated parser). C SQLite's parse.y (~1,900 productions, ~76KB) as authoritative grammar reference. Deliberate switch from Lemon LALR(1) for Rust ergonomics, error recovery, debuggability.

**Structure:** One method per grammar production. Key methods: parse_statement -> parse_select_stmt (with/core/compound/order/limit) -> parse_insert_stmt (upsert/returning) -> parse_update_stmt -> parse_delete_stmt -> parse_create_table_stmt (column_def/constraint) -> parse_create_index/view/trigger -> parse_drop/alter -> parse_begin/commit/rollback -> parse_pragma -> parse_explain -> parse_expr (Pratt).

**Pratt precedence table (11 levels):**
1. OR | 2. AND | 3. NOT (prefix) | 4. =,==,!=,<>,IS,IN,LIKE,GLOB,BETWEEN,MATCH,REGEXP,ISNULL,NOTNULL | 5. <,<=,>,>= | 6. &,|,<<,>> | 7. +,- | 8. *,/,% | 9. || (concat), ->, ->> | 10. COLLATE | 11. ~ (bitwise not), unary +/-

Key: equality/membership (4) and relational (5) at SEPARATE levels — `a = b < c` parses as `a = (b < c)`.

**ESCAPE note:** Not in Pratt dispatch table. Parsed as optional suffix of LIKE/GLOB handler.

**Error recovery:** Record error (token, expected, span) -> skip to sync point (semicolon/EOF/statement keyword) -> continue parsing -> return all errors with partial AST.

## §10.3 AST Node Types

**Statement enum:** Select, Insert, Update, Delete, CreateTable/Index/View/Trigger/VirtualTable, Drop, AlterTable, Begin, Commit, Rollback, Savepoint, Release, Attach, Detach, Pragma, Vacuum, Reindex, Analyze, Explain.

**SelectStatement:** with, body (SelectBody = SelectCore + compounds), order_by, limit.

**SelectCore enum:** Select { distinct, columns, from, where, group_by, having, windows } | Values(Vec<Vec<Expr>>) — VALUES is first-class construct.

**Expr enum (~15+ variants):** Literal, Column, BinaryOp, UnaryOp, Between, In, Like (with escape), Case, Cast, Exists, Subquery, FunctionCall (with filter + over), Collate, IsNull, Raise, JsonAccess, RowValue, Placeholder. All carry Span.

 {"id":"bd-1xds","title":"§17.3 Deterministic Concurrency Tests: Lab Runtime + FsLab Harness","description":"E2E Test:
- test_e2e_bd_1xds_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.

## SUMMARY
Implements deterministic concurrency testing using asupersync's lab runtime via the FsLab harness (§4.2.3). All MVCC tests run under deterministic scheduling with fixed seeds for exact reproducibility. The harness supports fault injection via FaultInjectingVfs, deterministic repro artifacts on failure, seed taxonomy (schedule/entropy/fault/fuzz seeds derived from root seed), and CI runs each concurrency test with 100 different seeds. Failing seeds produce self-contained repro bundles for one-command reproduction.

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS
- **FsLab:** `fsqlite_harness::lab::FsLab` wrapper around asupersync's lab runtime. Configured with seed, worker_count, and max_steps. Provides deterministic async task scheduling.
- **Seed Management:** Fixed seed per test for reproducibility. CI runs 100 different seeds per test. Failing seed recorded in test failure message.
- **Seed Taxonomy:** Root test_seed derives: schedule_seed, entropy_seed, fault_seed, fuzz_seed via `H(test_seed || purpose_tag || scope_id)` where H is xxh3_64 or SplitMix64 and purpose_tag is ASCII ("schedule", "entropy", "fault", "fuzz").
- **FaultInjectingVfs:** `fsqlite_harness::vfs::FaultInjectingVfs` wraps UnixVfs. Supports `FaultSpec::partial_write("test.db-wal").at_offset_bytes(4096).bytes_written(2048).after_count(50)` style injection.
- **Repro Artifacts:** On failure with `ASUPERSYNC_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR` set, emit: `repro_manifest.json`, `event_log.txt`, `failed_assertions.json`, optional `trace.async` and `inputs.bin`.
- **repro_manifest.json Schema:** schema_version, test_id, seed, scenario_id, config_hash, trace_fingerprint, input_digest, oracle_violations, passed.
- **Replay Workflow:** Load manifest, re-run with `ASUPERSYNC_SEED=<seed>`, if trace.async exists replay directly, divergence produces divergence artifact.
- **Oracle Report:** `report.oracle_report.all_passed()` assertion validates all MVCC invariants held.

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS
- INV-DET-1: Every concurrency test MUST use a fixed seed for exact reproducibility.
- INV-DET-2: CI MUST run each concurrency test with 100 different seeds.
- INV-DET-3: Failing seed MUST be recorded in the test failure message.
- INV-DET-4: When ASUPERSYNC_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR is set, any failing run MUST emit a self-contained repro bundle.
- INV-DET-5: Seed derivation MUST follow `H(test_seed || purpose_tag || scope_id)` with stable hash function.
- INV-DET-6: Replay with same seed and scenario MUST produce identical execution trace (determinism guarantee).
- INV-DET-7: repro_manifest.json MUST conform to the minimum schema (schema_version, test_id, seed, scenario_id, config_hash, trace_fingerprint, oracle_violations, passed).

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
- `test_mvcc_two_writers_different_pages`: T1 inserts rowids 1-100, T2 inserts rowids 1001-1100 concurrently. Both commit. Final count = 200. Uses seed 0xDEADBEEF, 4 workers, 200K max steps.
- `test_mvcc_two_writers_same_page_conflict`: Two transactions write to same page range. One must abort or both succeed if cells are disjoint (per merge ladder §5.10).
- `test_deterministic_replay_same_seed`: Run same scenario twice with identical seed. Verify execution traces are identical.
- `test_fault_injection_partial_wal_write`: Inject partial write at WAL offset 4096 after 50 writes. Verify database recovers correctly on next open.
- `test_repro_artifact_generation`: Set ASUPERSYNC_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR, run a test that fails by assertion. Verify repro_manifest.json, event_log.txt, and failed_assertions.json are emitted with correct schema.
- `test_seed_taxonomy_derivation`: Derive schedule_seed, entropy_seed, fault_seed, fuzz_seed from root seed. Verify deterministic and distinct.
- `test_ci_100_seeds_coverage`: Run a concurrency scenario with seeds 0..99. Verify all complete (pass or fail) and any failure records its seed.
- `test_fault_injection_io_error_on_read`: Inject I/O read failure, verify transaction aborts cleanly without corruption.
- `test_replay_divergence_detection`: Replay with tampered trace.async file, verify divergence artifact emitted at first mismatch.

- test_e2e_bd_1xds_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.
Run the full MVCC deterministic concurrency test suite under FsLab. Execute each test with 100 seeds in CI. Verify zero non-determinism: same seed always produces same outcome. Inject a deliberate fault (partial WAL write), verify recovery. Trigger a failing test, verify repro artifacts are emitted in the correct directory layout. Replay the failing test from the repro manifest and verify identical failure.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- FsLab harness operational: deterministic task scheduling with configurable workers and max_steps.
- All MVCC concurrency tests pass under deterministic scheduling with fixed seeds.
- FaultInjectingVfs supports partial writes, I/O errors, and configurable fault timing.
- CI runs 100 seeds per concurrency test; failing seeds recorded.
- Repro artifacts emitted on failure with correct schema and directory layout.
- Replay from repro manifest produces identical execution (no flakes).
- Seed taxonomy follows normative derivation rule.
- Oracle report validates all MVCC invariants for every seed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:04:51.778346311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:25:42.897806517Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:25:42.897768756Z","close_reason":"Added required bd-1xds unit/property/e2e compliance gates and logging marker coverage in harness tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xds","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1xds","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1xds","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":589,"issue_id":"bd-1xds","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §17.3 Deterministic Concurrency Tests — Lab Runtime (from P2 bd-1p0j)

All MVCC tests via asupersync lab runtime + FsLab wrapper.

**Fixed seed for reproducibility.** CI runs each concurrency test with 100 different seeds. Failing seed recorded in message.

**Deterministic repro artifacts:** On failure, emit bundle to $ASUPERSYNC_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR/{test_id}/: repro_manifest.json, event_log.txt, failed_assertions.json, optional trace.async + inputs.bin.

**Seed taxonomy:** test_seed (root), derived: schedule_seed, entropy_seed, fault_seed, fuzz_seed. Derivation: `H(test_seed || purpose_tag || scope_id)` with xxh3_64 or SplitMix64.

**repro_manifest.json schema:** schema_version, test_id, seed, scenario_id, config_hash, trace_fingerprint, input_digest, oracle_violations, passed.

**Replay workflow:** Load manifest -> re-run with ASUPERSYNC_SEED -> replay trace.async -> divergence artifact on mismatch.

**Fault injection:** FaultInjectingVfs with FaultSpec (partial_write, at_offset, after_count).
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:23:04Z"},{"id":590,"issue_id":"bd-1xds","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §17.3 Deterministic Concurrency Tests: Lab Runtime

### Spec Content (Lines 16403-16510)

All MVCC tests run under asupersync's lab runtime via fsqlite-harness's FsLab wrapper (Section 4.2.3).

**Setup pattern:** Create FsLab with fixed seed (e.g., 0xDEADBEEF), worker_count(4), max_steps(200_000). Run async closure that opens in-memory database, creates table, spawns concurrent writer tasks (e.g., "writer.low" and "writer.high"), executes transactions, awaits completion, verifies results via query. Assert oracle_report.all_passed().

**Seed management:** Each test uses a fixed seed for reproducibility. CI runs each concurrency test with 100 different seeds. A failing seed is recorded in the test failure message for exact replay.

**Deterministic repro artifacts (asupersync-native):**
When ASUPERSYNC_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR is set, failing deterministic lab runs MUST emit self-contained repro bundles:
- Directory layout: {test_id}/repro_manifest.json, event_log.txt, failed_assertions.json, trace.async (optional), inputs.bin (optional)
- Seed taxonomy: test_seed (root), derived seeds: schedule_seed, entropy_seed, fault_seed, fuzz_seed
- Derivation rule: derived = H(test_seed || purpose_tag || scope_id) where H is xxh3_64 or SplitMix64
- repro_manifest.json minimum schema: schema_version, test_id, seed, scenario_id, config_hash, trace_fingerprint, input_digest, oracle_violations, passed

**Replay workflow:** Load repro_manifest.json, re-run with ASUPERSYNC_SEED=<seed>, replay trace.async if exists (divergence produces divergence artifact).

**Fault injection:** Lab reactor supports injecting I/O failures:
- FaultInjectingVfs wrapping UnixVfs
- FaultSpec::partial_write("test.db-wal").at_offset_bytes(4096).bytes_written(2048).after_count(50)

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_lab_two_writers_different_pages: Two concurrent writers inserting into different rowid ranges, both commit, total count = 200
2. test_lab_fixed_seed_reproducibility: Same seed produces identical execution trace across 10 runs
3. test_lab_100_seeds_ci: Run two-writer test with 100 different seeds, all pass
4. test_lab_failing_seed_recorded: Intentionally failing test records failing seed in error message
5. test_lab_repro_manifest_schema: Failing test emits repro_manifest.json with all required fields (schema_version, test_id, seed, scenario_id, config_hash, trace_fingerprint, oracle_violations, passed)
6. test_lab_event_log_emitted: Failing test emits event_log.txt
7. test_lab_failed_assertions_json: Failing test emits failed_assertions.json
8. test_lab_seed_derivation_xxh3: Derived seeds use H(test_seed || purpose_tag || scope_id) with xxh3_64
9. test_lab_seed_taxonomy: Repro manifest records schedule_seed, entropy_seed, fault_seed, fuzz_seed
10. test_lab_replay_from_manifest: Load repro_manifest.json and re-run with ASUPERSYNC_SEED produces identical failure
11. test_lab_trace_replay_divergence: Replay with modified code produces divergence artifact with first mismatched event
12. test_lab_fault_injection_partial_write: FaultInjectingVfs partial_write at offset, database recovers correctly
13. test_lab_fault_injection_after_count: Fault triggers after N operations, not before
14. test_lab_worker_count_4: Lab runs with worker_count(4) and max_steps(200_000) without exceeding step budget

### E2E Test
End-to-end validation: Run a complex MVCC scenario (100 threads inserting into separate rowid ranges) under FsLab deterministic scheduling. Execute with 100 different seeds via CI. For any failing seed, verify repro bundle is emitted with complete repro_manifest.json (seed taxonomy, oracle_violations, config_hash). Replay the failing seed from the manifest and confirm identical failure. Inject I/O faults (partial writes to WAL) and verify crash recovery under deterministic scheduling. Verify that trace fingerprints are stable across replays with the same seed.

 {"id":"bd-1xfri","title":"S3-FIFO Buffer Pool Eviction (§15.1)","description":"Replace current buffer pool eviction with S3-FIFO (Yang et al. 2023), the state-of-the-art eviction algorithm. Score 8.0 in the cross-project scoreboard. S3-FIFO consistently beats LRU, LRU-K, ARC, and LIRS across diverse workloads while being simpler and more cache-friendly.

## Background
Buffer pool eviction determines which pages stay in memory when the pool is full. LRU (Least Recently Used) is the textbook approach but suffers from scan pollution (a single sequential scan evicts all hot pages) and poor performance on skewed access patterns. ARC (Adaptive Replacement Cache) improves on LRU but requires per-access metadata updates that contend under concurrency.

## Algorithm: S3-FIFO
Three queues:
1. SMALL queue (10% of total capacity): FIFO admission filter. New pages enter here.
2. MAIN queue (90% of capacity): FIFO with frequency-based reinsertion. Pages promoted from SMALL if accessed again.
3. GHOST queue (tracks recently evicted pages): No data, just page IDs. Used for frequency estimation.

Eviction logic:
- Evict from SMALL first (FIFO order). If evicted page was accessed while in SMALL, promote to MAIN instead.
- If SMALL is empty, evict from MAIN (FIFO order). If evicted page was accessed while in MAIN, reinsert at tail.
- Posterior-guided adaptive split: adjust the SMALL/MAIN ratio based on observed hit rates (Bayesian update on which queue contributes more hits).

## Why S3-FIFO
- Lock-free friendly: FIFO queues only touch head/tail, not middle (unlike LRU which moves accessed items)
- Scan resistant: sequential scans fill SMALL queue and get evicted without polluting MAIN
- Adaptive: posterior-guided split handles workload changes

## Key Paper
Yang, Yue, Rashmi, et al. 2023: 'FIFO Queues are All You Need for Cache Eviction'

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 3-queue S3-FIFO structure implemented
- [ ] Posterior-guided adaptive queue split
- [ ] Lock-free or low-contention implementation
- [ ] Hit rate improvement over LRU on mixed workloads
- [ ] Scan resistance demonstrated
- [ ] Benchmark: S3-FIFO vs LRU vs ARC hit rates on trace-driven workload

## Success Criteria
